channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: A convincing Google Pixel 9a hands-on video has mysteriously disappeared, but I think it could’ve been the real thing
item.description: A YouTuber released, and subsequently pulled, a short vertical video of a hands-on with the Google Pixel 9a, and it’s probably the best look at the new mid-range handset we've had so far.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Musk business entanglements loom over CFPB siege
item.description: Elon Musk’s plans to add payment services to X, his social media platform, are looming large over the Trump administration’s push to dismantle the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB), The tech billionaire has long voiced his hopes of turning X into an “everything app” that would also functio
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: The 8BitDo Ultimate is my favorite Nintendo Switch controller, and now it's finally getting an upgraded version
item.description: The 8BitDo Ultimate 2 is an upgrade of my favorite Nintendo Switch controller for Windows and Android devices.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: What gives IT leaders pause as they look to integrate agentic AI with legacy infrastructure
item.description: Agentic AI was the big breakthrough technology for gen AI last year, and this year, enterprises will deploy these systems at scale.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: INE Secures Spot in G2’s 2025 Top 50 Education Software Rankings
item.description: INE, the leading provider of networking and cybersecurity training and certifications, today announced its recognition as an enterprise and small business leader in online course providers and cybersecurity professional development, along with its designation as the recipient of G2’s 2025 Best Softw
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Elon Musk’s DOGE is draining the life from the once-vaunted U.S. Digital Service
item.description: The United States Digital Service (USDS), the storied group of Silicon Valley types brought together by Obama to bring government services into the 21st century, will likely never be the same after the arrival of Elon Musk’s so-called Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE).
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Compute Exchange aims to disrupt AI compute access with auction-based platform
item.description: Companies looking for additional compute capacity for their AI initiatives at a price they can afford now have another option. Compute Exchange offers what it calls the world’s first auction-based exchange for AI compute resources.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Cot framework aims to ease Rust web development
item.description: Web developers looking for an easier time with the Rust language may want to check out Cot, an emerging web framework pitched as being for “lazy developers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Kate Bush, Annie Lennox, and a slew of other stars are on this silent album. Here’s why
item.description: A new album called “Is This What We Want?” features a stellar list of more than 1,000 musicians—and the sound of silence.
item.dc:creator: azapata, Fast Company

item.title: Salesforce: Latest news and insights
item.description: Salesforce is a vendor of cloud-based software and applications for sales, customer service, marketing automation, ecommerce, analytics, and application development. Based in San Francisco, Calif.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Former VA cybersecurity official warns DOGE access threatens sensitive data
item.description: A former Department of Veterans Affairs cybersecurity official warned that the Department of Government Efficiency’s (DOGE) access to the department’s information threatens sensitive data stored within the organization.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: The European Center for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts Unveils New AI Model
item.description: The ECMWF introduces AIFS, an Artificial Intelligence Forecasting System that can predict medium-range weather conditions with improved accuracy and efficiency.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Tesla says it will refresh the Model S and Model X this year – here are 5 things I want to see
item.description: Tesla's Vice President of Vehicle Engineering lets slip that the Model X and Model will receive "some love" this year.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: 21 staffers in office overtaken by DOGE resign
item.description: More than 20 staffers who worked in the federal department overtaken by the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) resigned Tuesday, saying they won’t use their technical skills to “jeopardize” Americans’ data and “compromise” essential government systems.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: What an irony! AMD could potentially block Intel deal by invoking agreement put in place to prevent its own sale
item.description: If Broadcom or another buyer does want to acquire Intel, they will have to negotiate a new cross-license agreement with AMD.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: The Music Industry's Hidden Billion: How Kobalt's Kosign Aims to Empower Emerging Songwriters
item.description: Kobalt's new platform, Kosign, is designed to help emerging songwriters collect their royalties without committing to a long-term contract.
item.dc:creator: Julia Selinger, Fast Company

item.title: 2025 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 5 time-saving Alexa commands you’re probably not using yet
item.description: Even if you’re a regular Alexa user, there’s a good chance you haven’t discovered some of its most efficient features. Actually, strike that: There’s a good chance you’re only using your Alexa device to set timers and play music.
item.dc:creator: Doug Aamoth, Fast Company

item.title: Alibaba to invest $52 billion in AI and cloud over three years
item.description: Alibaba is planning to invest 380 billion yuan ($52 billion) in artificial intelligence and cloud computing over the next three years, its biggest commitment yet to AI-driven infrastructure. That’s more than its total spending on AI and cloud computing over the past decade, it said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How VR technology is curing loneliness in seniors
item.description: VR transforms senior care: Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says immersive tech helps older adults explore and connect via virtual experiences.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Potential cuts at AI Safety Institute stoke concerns in tech industry
item.description: Potential cuts to the U.S. AI Safety Institute (AISI) are causing alarm among some in the technology space who fear the development of responsible artificial intelligence (AI) could be at risk as President Trump works to downsize the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: How to use Gemini AI to write (and rewrite) in Google Docs and Gmail
item.description: Whether you’re a professional writer or someone who dreads having to compose documents or emails for your job, the potential of AI assistance for your writing tasks is appealing.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: What the private sector can teach government about cloud computing
item.description: Cloud computing has been at the forefront of government IT strategies for many years. When cloud computing first became a thing in 2008, the government led the way, including NIST creating a definition of cloud computing. I worked closely with them then and watched the whole thing play out.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: JDK 24: The new features in Java 24
item.description: Java Development Kit (JDK) 24 has entered the final release candidate stage. The next version of standard Java will ship with 24 features, ranging from a class-file API to a fourth preview of structured concurrency.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Apple shareholders reject proposal to scrap company's diversity programs
item.description: Apple shareholders rebuffed an attempt to pressure the technology trendsetter into joining President Donald Trump’s push to scrub corporate programs designed to diversify its workforce.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: VA EHR office shrinks headcount under Trump’s federal workforce policies
item.description: VA officials are working on an updated EHR deployment schedule beyond the Michigan go-lives in mid-2026. The post VA EHR office shrinks headcount under Trump’s federal workforce policies first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: Protecting US supply chains from foreign influence
item.description: With the new Trump administration likely to place an even greater focus on reducing reliance on China, this trend is expected to accelerate. The post Protecting US supply chains from foreign influence first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Top Apple exec concedes he had doubts about app store fees imposed on alternative payment options
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — Longtime Apple executive Phil Schiller on Monday conceded he initially opposed fees that the iPhone maker charges for in app-transactions processed outside its own payment system because he feared violating a court-ordered mandate designed to create more competition.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Elizabeth Holmes fails to overturn her Theranos fraud conviction
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Elizabeth Holmes, the disgraced founder of Theranos, will remain in prison after losing a bid Monday to overturn her fraud conviction, with a federal appeals court saying she hadn’t proved there were legal missteps during her trial for defrauding investors with false claims of w
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: MongoDB acquires Voyage AI to reduce hallucinations in AI applications
item.description: Database engine developer MongoDB has acquired Voyage AI in order to help enterprises reduce hallucinations in AI-powered applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: This is what no one planned could happen after taking a DNA test
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says DNA tests pose privacy risks like shared genetic information, inaccuracies and hard data deletion.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Apple looks to inject $500B into US economy
item.description: Apple plans to inject billions of dollars into the US economy, spending $500 billion in the US over the next four years and promising expansion, new manufacturing facilities, and big investments in advanced manufacturing and R&D.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Massive airship could shake up cargo transport
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says this enormous airship revolutionizes cargo transport by flying to remote areas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GenAI’s unexpected impact: Disrupting high-skilled tech jobs, too
item.description: A new Brookings Institution report on generative AI (genAI) found that the more highly skilled a tech worker is, the more vulnerable they are to having their jobs supplemented by the technology.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Can you spot a credit card skimmer? What to look for
item.description: Before putting your card into a reader, the FBI says there are some easy and effective ways to protect your information.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Harrison Ford addresses AI fears
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Brazil Supreme Court justice orders Rumble suspension nationwide for alleged non-compliance
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — A Brazilian Supreme Court justice on Friday ordered the suspension of video-sharing platform Rumble in the South American nation for allegedly refusing to comply with court orders.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Sweden is investigating a damaged cable in the Baltic Sea
item.description: STOCKHOLM (AP) — Swedish authorities said on Friday they were investigating a damaged cable that was discovered in the Baltic Sea, the latest in a string of recent incidents of ruptured undersea cables that have heightened fears of Russian sabotage and spying in the region.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: GenAI is my copilot? Developers weigh pains and gains
item.description: Developers are quicker than most to embrace generative AI tools as part of their workflow. That can mean enhanced productivity and easing cognitive burdens, but it also means learning new technologies while they’re still being built.
item.dc:creator: Info World
